skill.name: quant-analyst
skill.description: Build financial models, backtest trading strategies, and analyze market data with risk metrics, portfolio optimization, and statistical arbitrage methods.
skill.summary: Analyze trading strategies, portfolio risk, and quantitative finance models.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and were dismissed as false positives. The backtick pattern is Markdown formatting for a file path, the crypto finding has no matching cryptographic usage, and the reconnaissance finding is financial testing language.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown File Reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 26 uses backticks to format resources/implementation-playbook.md as inline Markdown. No shell execution, Ruby execution, or external command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only a Markdown-formatted resource path. There is no command syntax or executable context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 3 describes building financial models and backtesting strategies. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security-sensitive crypto usage appears on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported line is plain skill metadata text about quantitative finance. It contains no evidence of weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 42 recommends out-of-sample testing to avoid model overfitting. This is a quantitative finance validation method, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The phrase is used in a modeling workflow context. No host, network, process, or environment enumeration is requested.

content.user_title: Analyze Quant Strategies and Market Risk
content.value_statement: Quantitative finance work needs careful assumptions, clean data, and disciplined validation. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure models, backtests, and risk reviews.
actual_capabilities: Plans trading strategy research and backtesting workflows.
Applies risk metrics such as VaR, Sharpe ratio, and max drawdown.
Guides portfolio optimization using Markowitz and Black-Litterman methods.
Supports time series analysis, forecasting, and options Greeks review.
Frames statistical arbitrage and pairs trading analysis.
Emphasizes transaction costs, slippage, and out-of-sample validation.
limitations: Does not provide live market data by itself.
Does not guarantee profitable trading results.
Requires the user to supply data, constraints, and assumptions.
Should not replace regulated financial advice or compliance review.

use_cases.title: Backtest a Trading Idea
use_cases.description: Turn a strategy concept into a test plan with assumptions, metrics, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Review Portfolio Risk
use_cases.description: Assess exposure, drawdown, volatility, and risk-adjusted return for a portfolio.

use_cases.title: Plan Market Data Analysis
use_cases.description: Design a clean workflow for market data ingestion, validation, and time series modeling.

prompt_templates.title: Define a Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define a trading strategy hypothesis. Ask for the asset universe, data frequency, signal logic, costs, and risk limits.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Backtest
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a backtest plan for this strategy. Include assumptions, transaction costs, slippage, performance metrics, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Portfolio Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this portfolio for volatility, drawdown, VaR, Sharpe ratio, factor exposure, and concentration risk.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Statistical Arbitrage
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this pairs trading idea. Cover stationarity, cointegration, entry rules, exit rules, sizing, and out-of-sample validation.

output_examples.input: I want to test a moving average strategy on liquid equities.
output: Defines the asset universe, lookback windows, rebalance frequency, and transaction cost assumptions.
Recommends performance metrics such as Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, turnover, and hit rate.
Adds walk-forward and out-of-sample checks before interpreting results.

output_examples.input: Review risk for a portfolio that is concentrated in technology stocks.
output: Highlights sector concentration, volatility, drawdown, and correlation risk.
Suggests factor exposure analysis and stress scenarios.
Separates risk observations from investment recommendations.

output_examples.input: Help me evaluate a pairs trading signal.
output: Checks whether the spread is stable and suitable for mean reversion analysis.
Defines entry, exit, stop, sizing, and holding period assumptions.
Warns against overfitting and requests out-of-sample validation.

best_practices: State data sources, time range, costs, and slippage before reviewing results.
Separate research results from production trading decisions.
Use out-of-sample testing and sensitivity analysis for every strategy.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize parameters only on historical returns.
Do not ignore data quality, survivorship bias, or look-ahead bias.
Do not treat model output as regulated financial advice.

faq.question: Does this skill fetch live market data?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure analysis, but the user must provide data or connect approved data tools.

faq.question: Can it create backtest code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide implementations using pandas, numpy, and scipy when coding tools are available.

faq.question: Does it provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It supports quantitative analysis and research workflows, not personalized financial advice.

faq.question: What risk metrics does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers metrics such as VaR, Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, volatility, exposure, and sensitivity.

faq.question: Can it help with portfolio optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. It can frame Markowitz and Black-Litterman workflows with constraints and assumptions.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
